title: jasione amethystina subsp. centralis
intro: Jasione amethystina subsp. centralis, also known as Central Amethyst Bluebells, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family (Campanulaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Jasione amethystina subsp. centralis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
